projects.name: Columbia Licensing Meeting

notes.body:
Store of the future

  • What’s their main story? Main direction.

  • Columbia Boys: Where does it work?
  • Columbia golf categories and distribution
notes.title: QUESTIONS

notes.body:
1. The "Women’s" Growth Engine

The most striking metric in the data is the disparity in growth between divisions. While the men's segment is stable at 8%, women’s is surging at 20%.

  • The Point: As we move toward Fall '27, there is a clear mandate to lean into the women's category to capture this momentum. We should be looking at how "Amaze" and "ROC" logic can be applied to further capture this demographic.

2. Transitioning to "Urban Relevance"

There is a strategic pivot toward making the brand "cooler" in urban centers and for urban retailers (like DSG and Scheels).

  • The Point: The design language is shifting from purely rugged/outdoor to "Elevated Utility" and "Technically Fashionable." This means performance features need to be discreet—think "hidden tech" rather than "overt tech." The goal is to make the product credible on the trail but stylish enough for the city.

3. Performance Powerhouses: ROC & Amaze

The growth numbers for the ROC (+106%) and Amaze (+208%) collections are massive.

  • The Point: These aren't just successful products; they are the new blueprints. The fact that the ROC pant is moving at a higher price point ($90) without being on sale shows that consumers are willing to pay for quality and fit (specifically the "Rockband Waistband"). We should double down on these "Fabric Platforms" rather than one-off styles.

4. The Year-Round PHG Shift

2027 marks the first year that PHG (Hunting) will be a year-round offering.

  • The Point: This is a significant operational and merchandising change. We need to focus on "Washed Down Camo" and "Saddle Point 2.0" to ensure the transition from a seasonal niche to a year-round lifestyle category is seamless, particularly in the farm and fleet channels like Tractor Supply.

5. The "October 15" North Star

Looking at the calendar, the timeline is tight.

  • The Point: We have a hard deadline for COPA Approval by October 15, 2026. This is the critical gatekeeper for the Sales Meetings in late October. To stay on track, the internal "First Adopt" needs to be ironclad by mid-June.
notes.title: KEY MEETING TAKEAWAYS

notes.body:
Columbia Sportswear: Strategy, Design & Performance Notes

Core Brand Identity

  • Defining Principle: Performance Obsessed.

  • Pro Line: Titanium (The premier/pro-level offering).

Apparel Design & Macro Trends

  • Wilderness Wellness: Focus on health and the outdoors.

  • Play Everyday: Versatility for daily activity.

  • Technically Fashionable: Merging high-performance specs with aesthetic appeal.

4 Key Design Trends:

  1. Elevated Utility: Adaptive, functional, and discreet performance features.

  2. Ease State: Performance-to-lifestyle transition; relaxed bottoms continue to trend.

  3. Sets: From ski-wear to sweats, coordinated sets remain a major statement.

  4. Return to Color: Focused on "Nostalgic Joy" and "Nature As" palettes.

Fabric Platforms

  • Primary Goal: Warm, Dry, Protected.

  • Soft Touch: Sensorial, soft, and comforting textures.

  • Amaze Puff: Fabric availability for Men’s and Boys'.

  • Hard Work: Protective, functional, and durable materials.

  • ROC (Rigged Outdoor Collection): Exploring belts working directly with this collection; bridges the gap between outdoor performance and rugged workwear.

Seasonal Color & Palettes

  • Q3 (Autumn Trails): Early Fall.

  • Q4 (Winter Dreams): Peak Winter.

  • Volume Drivers: Pond, Mystery, and Nocturnal Red represent the "Dollar" colors.

  • Core Base: Black accounts for 70% of the assortment.

Sub-Brand Focus:

  • PFG (Performance Fishing Gear): Assortment shrinks in Fall. Palette is "Fair Winds"—dusty tones, though brights remain essential. Key colors: Siberia, Bold Orange, Dockside Blue.

  • PHG (Performance Hunting Gear): Additional deck updates pending.

Patterns & Consumer Macro Trends

  • Countryside Craft: Handcrafted, rustic aesthetics.

  • Camo Mash: Mixing and layering different camouflage patterns.

  • Plaid Mania: Heavy trend focus on checks.

  • Ornamental Nature: Decorative animal and nature-inspired prints.

  • Hierarchy: Clear "Good/Better/Best" distinctions across the range.

Business Strategy & Pillars

Strategic Pillars:

  • Own the Trail

  • Dominate Warm

  • Power PFG

  • Fuel Outdoor Lifestyle

  • Accelerate Footwear

Growth Strategy ("Next Level"):

  • Core Strength: Focus on the "Everyday Enthusiast" and "Sensitive Style."

  • Key Market: China (Current leader in the active/outdoor space).

  • 3 Drivers: Credibility, Relevance, Style.

  • Urban Reach: Must increase brand "cool factor" and relevance in urban retail centers.

Performance Results & Metrics

  • ROC Collection: +106% growth ($90 price point, high retention, "Rockband Waistband").

  • Amaze Collection: +208% growth.

  • Product Categories: Dry (+10%), Cool (+37%), Omni-Max Footwear (+55%), Sun Protection (+10%).

  • Demographics: Women’s growth (20%) significantly outpacing Men’s (8%).

    • Market Share: Men’s 48%, Women’s 40%, Youth 8%.

Fall 2027 Planning

  • Fuel Outdoor Lifestyle: Amaze Men’s launching; addition of Amaze Sweats, Youth, and Accessories (Faux Fur Bomber).

  • Timberline Tough: Expansion of rugged jackets and parkas.

  • Fishing/Hunting:

    • PFG: Rolling out "Second Layer & Terminal Tech" through F27.

    • PHG: Moving to a year-round model in 2027. Key trend: "Washed Down Camo" (Soft hand knits). Focus on the Farm channel (Tractor Supply) and Spring goods (Academy).

Merchandising & Retail (North America)

  • Wholesale: Focus on DSG (Dick's Sporting Goods) and Scheels for growth.

  • DTC Footprint: 187 total locations (150 Outlets, 25 Branded Stores, 6 Employee Stores, 2 E-comm).

  • E-commerce: Content engagement is up +56% YoY. Strategy focuses on site personalization and high-volume search terms like "Stocking Stuffers."

Fall 2027 Calendar

MilestoneDate RangeLine Plan Review02/01/26 – 02/28/26COPA Approval06/15/26 – 10/15/26 (Final Target)First Adopt (Internal)06/19/26Assortment Presentations10/15/26 – 12/31/26IDR / Sales Meeting10/23/26

Context: Alex Ferreira / Hotdog Hans

  • Alex Ferreira: A two-time Olympic medalist and X Games gold medalist in freestyle skiing.

  • Hotdog Hans: Ferreira’s viral alter ego—a "crusty old man" character who pranks younger skiers by performing professional-level tricks while dressed in vintage gear and denim. Columbia sponsors the content series, leaning into the "relevance" and "style" pillars mentioned in your notes.
notes.title: Fall 2027 Design Columbia
